narratives.Description: Port scripts/pr_merge_readiness.py -- the merge-readiness primitive that parses Greptile findings, computes the confidence verdict, applies the layered fallbacks (#1368), and returns the three-state readiness result -- to TypeScript. Subprocess capture uses Node execFile (no shell) with explicit UTF-8 decoding, structurally closing the #1366 capture-decode class. It reuses the @deftai/core/scm gh helpers and is the foundation the pr_wait_mergeable cascade composer builds on; dispatched first.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Create the packages/core/src/pr-merge-readiness module porting the Greptile-findings parse, confidence verdict, and layered fallbacks over Node execFile UTF-8-safe capture, reusing @deftai/core/scm for gh calls, with vitest unit tests in the module directory.
2. Add packages/cli/src/pr-merge-readiness.ts exposing the TS readiness verb behind the existing CLI seam.
3. Add packages/cli/src/pr-merge-readiness-parity.ts that runs the TS path and the Python scripts/pr_merge_readiness.py oracle and asserts byte-identical output with cache off.
narratives.UserStory: As a directive maintainer, I want the PR merge-readiness check available in TypeScript, so that the Greptile-findings verdict and layered fallbacks run on the TS engine with the same output as the Python module and UTF-8-safe capture by default.

narrative.Acceptance: Running the TS merge-readiness check returns the same three-state verdict and findings counts the Python pr_merge_readiness.py module produces for the same PR fixture.
Running the readiness check against a fixture whose gh output carries non-ASCII bytes returns the parsed verdict and emits no decode error, because capture uses execFile with explicit UTF-8.
Running the pr-merge-readiness-parity binary returns exit 0 and renders byte-identical output to the Python scripts/pr_merge_readiness.py oracle with cache off.

expected_outputs: New packages/core/src/pr-merge-readiness module with vitest unit tests
Byte-identical parity vs scripts/pr_merge_readiness.py under cache-off
swarm.missing_traces_justification: #1530 Wave-4b framework-internal TS port traced to GitHub issue #1730 and umbrella epic #1530; no specification.vbrief.json spec-section applies to maintainer tooling.

references.title: Issue #1730: feat(ts-migration): port pr_* monitors + cascade automation to TS (Wave 4)
